original_question:
This SVG path element <path d="M 40.56,25.73 L 45.83,31.92 M 45.83,31.92 L 38.73,33.06 M 38.73,33.06 L 33.00,28.70 M 33.00,28.70 L 40.56,25.73"/> draws a
Options:

(F) octagon

(H) rectangle
(I) sector

subquestion:
Mask out the bottom-right corner of the following SVG path element, which represents a rectangle, and ask me to fill in the missing text to make the resulting shape a false instance of the concept of a rectangle. 

M 10.00,10.00 L 15.00,15.00 M 15.00,15.00 L 20.00,10.00 M 20.00,10.00 L 15.00,15.00
judge_answer:
To create a false instance of a rectangle, we need to create an odd-shaped polygon. The given path already forms a triangle, so to make it a false rectangle, we need to add a line that closes the shape. 

The current path forms a triangle by connecting the points (10,10), (15,15), and (20,10). To create a false rectangle, we need to add a line that closes the shape by connecting the point (20,10) to a new point (10,0). This will create a shape that has four sides, but is not a rectangle because it is not a parallelogram.

The missing text to make the resulting shape a false instance of the concept of a rectangle is M 20.00,10.00 L 10.00,0.

FINAL ANSWER: correct
